title: Outcome Measurement, Baselines, Guardrails, and Evaluation Design · Product Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for outcome measurement, baselines, guardrails, and evaluation design and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for outcome evaluation and measurement protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model inputs, activities, outputs, short and long outcomes, assumptions, external factors, and possible negative pathways.
Translate each construct into observable measure, population, unit, collection method, timing, uncertainty, and interpretation limit.
Choose historical, within-subject, benchmark, cohort, staged, qualitative, or build-nothing comparison appropriate to the claim.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Analysis Precommitment
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Impact is inferred from vanity metrics after launch without a baseline, comparison, guardrail, subgroup view, or precommitted interpretation. The quiz variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Precommit inclusion, exclusion, missing-data, success, stopping, and interpretation rules before seeing outcome data.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Analysis Precommitment.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for precommit inclusion, exclusion, missing-data, success, stopping, and interpretation rules before seeing outcome data. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Analysis Precommitment, keep Analysis Precommitment connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Impact is inferred from vanity metrics after launch without a baseline, comparison, guardrail, subgroup view, or precommitted interpretation. The quiz variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Precommit inclusion, exclusion, missing-data, success, stopping, and interpretation rules before seeing outcome data.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Analysis Precommitment symptom in outcome evaluation and measurement protocol during this quiz case.

The quiz variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Precommit inclusion, exclusion, missing-data, success, stopping, and interpretation rules before seeing outcome data.

For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Analysis Precommitment, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Changing an analysis after seeing results is harmless exploration.

stimulus.title: Analysis Precommitment failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Changing an analysis after seeing results is harmless exploration.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Precommit inclusion, exclusion, missing-data, success, stopping, and interpretation rules before seeing outcome data.

options.text: Run the Analysis Precommitment boundary case for outcome evaluation and measurement protocol, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the Analysis Precommitment result because visible outcome evaluation and measurement protocol output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Analysis Precommitment.

For diagnose the hidden failure, apply Analysis Precommitment without its stated preconditions, omit the outcome evaluation and measurement protocol trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Analysis Precommitment for outcome evaluation and measurement protocol.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Theory Of Change
steps.instruction:
Order the Theory Of Change evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Theory Of Change.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Theory Of Change assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Theory Of Change, keep Theory Of Change connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Analysis Precommitment
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Analysis Precommitment: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Impact is inferred from vanity metrics after launch without a baseline, comparison, guardrail, subgroup view, or precommitted interpretation. The quiz variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Precommit inclusion, exclusion, missing-data, success, stopping, and interpretation rules before seeing outcome data.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Analysis Precommitment.
steps.why: Retrieving Analysis Precommitment through the concrete outcome evaluation and measurement protocol case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Analysis Precommitment, keep Analysis Precommitment connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Precommit inclusion, exclusion, missing-data, success, stopping, and interpretation rules before seeing outcome data.
